source: Item 17 Franchise Agreement + Operator's Lease provision tables (Item 17, pages 39–42); Item 6 rent mechanics (pages 18–24); Item 10 BOA financing (page 30); Item 11 training (pages 33–36); Item 8 supply (pages 26–28). Exhibit B (traditional Franchise Agreement) itself not independently parsed; burden language captured from the FDD's own summaries plus cited sections.

grant_and_term.grant_scope: Limited right to use McDonald's System at one specific address — no territory, no development rights, no brand-extension rights

fee_and_payment_obligations.rent_structure: Monthly Base + Pass Thru + Percentage Rent; Percentage Rent 6%–23% (11.50% floor from year 8)

site_selection_and_construction.franchisee_role: cannot select or approve site; cannot sign FA until site selected

operational_obligations: provide monthly financial statements via FFS
accept periodic operations visits and inspections

material_breach_list: failure to maintain restaurant in good, clean, wholesome manner per McDonald's standards
any amount owing to franchisor unpaid >30 days
unsatisfied judgment >$5,000 against you for >30 days
loss of right of possession of restaurant
knowing sale of foods other than those approved or failing McDonald's standards
transfer of franchise without McDonald's prior consent
failure to make prompt payment of undisputed invoices
misrepresentation relating to ownership or acquisition of franchise
termination_triggers_by_franchisor.curable_defaults_general: not enumerated in FA summary; 10-day cure applies under Operator's Lease §7.04 for defaults other than payment/reporting/abandonment
termination_triggers_by_franchisor.noncurable_defaults: per §§18, 19 — several triggers above are structurally non-curable

transfer_restrictions.definition: Any direct, indirect, or contingent transfer of any interest in the franchise
transfer_restrictions.royalty_stepup_on_transfer: new transferee pays current (5%) royalty except for legacy exceptions
transfer_restrictions.death_disability_option_trigger: franchisor has managed restaurant for 1 year after death/disability

default_and_cross_default_provisions.source: Item 10 — default on BOA-program loan is considered a default under the Franchise Agreement; franchisee must then sign Operator Assistance Program Agreement (Exhibit N)

noncompete.in_term_scope: no involvement in any competing or similar business

state_overrides.washington: no-poach provisions removed per Washington AG Assurance of Discontinuance

liquidated_damages: Not explicitly summarized in Item 17. Termination remedy is franchisor's option to purchase FF&E at fair market value (no payment for intangibles) per §20.

venue_and_dispute_resolution.dispute_resolution_summary: Marked 'Not Applicable' in Item 17(u/v/w) for Franchise Agreement and Operator's Lease
venue_and_dispute_resolution.special_risks_page: Requires resolution of disputes — mediation, arbitration and/or litigation — only in Illinois

state_overrides.hawaii: anti-waiver language: no acknowledgement may waive claims under applicable state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
state_overrides.maryland: MD-law claims may be brought in MD subject to arbitration/FAA; 3-year limitations period
state_overrides.minnesota: Minn. Stat. 80C.14 notice periods supersede (90 days termination/60 cure; 180 days non-renewal; reasonable transfer consent)
state_overrides.north_dakota: ND law governs ND Franchise Investment Law claims
state_overrides.washington: RCW 19.100.180 / WA FIPA supremacy where Act applies

insurance_requirements.carrier_rating_minimum: A+ or A per A.M. Best

indemnification_obligations.scope: Franchisee indemnifies franchisor; no reciprocal franchisor defense of franchisee on trademark or copyright claims

force_majeure.item_11_references: Restaurant opening may be delayed by weather, delayed delivery/installation, labor disputes, governmental regulation, or other causes beyond franchisor's reasonable control. Franchisee cannot open for business until FA executed and initial fee paid.

distinctive_or_unusual_clauses: Franchisor owns or head-leases the real estate and mandates the Operator's Lease as part of the franchise. This is not typical of most QSR franchise systems — McDonald's is as much a landlord as a franchisor.

Due-to-parent inter-company balance ($3.31B) and 2% parent royalty expense create a structural upstream of nearly all net income to the parent. The Company's retained earnings are held constant at $4.63B year over year.
Sesame POS is the mandatory platform and is owned by the parent (McDonald's Corporation), not McDonald's USA, LLC.
Monthly financial statement reporting obligation (FFS) goes beyond typical QSR disclosure — full balance sheet + G&A + debt summary + per-restaurant P&L.
Confidentiality clause in Item 20 explicitly acknowledges that some franchisees are contractually restricted from speaking openly about their experience.
Franchisor holds uncapped independent access to transaction-level POS and cashless data — no contractual limit on use.
In-term non-compete broadly prohibits any involvement in any competing or similar business — not just ownership but any involvement.
Co-investment 'Quarters' mechanism allows rent reduction in 0.25% increments at $30K per Quarter; a unique McDonald's-specific rent-sharing feature.

Item 7 Note 11 discloses that 9 of 31 2024 McOpCo sales exceeded the published initial investment high end by up to $2.04M — explicit franchisor acknowledgement that the published range understates reality for the upper half of refranchising.
Washington AOD is a rare disclosed state AG enforcement action on no-poach.
